mcq.q: The layer of gases around Earth is called the…
mcq.explain: The atmosphere contains the air we breathe and protects Earth.

mcq.q: Climate change is caused mainly by…
mcq.explain: Burning fossil fuels releases CO₂, trapping heat.

choices: Heat from the sun near Earth
mcq.explain: Greenhouse gases let sunlight in but trap heat going out.

mcq.q: Which gas is a major greenhouse gas?
mcq.explain: CO₂ is released when fossil fuels burn.

mcq.q: A way YOU can reduce your carbon footprint is…
mcq.explain: Less fuel use = less CO₂.
Warmer oceans strengthen hurricanes; melting ice raises sea levels.

flashcards.back: Total greenhouse gas a person causes

lessons.text: Let's explore: Environment & Climate

mcq.explain: Blood carries oxygen, nutrients and takes waste to be removed.

mcq.q: Which organ filters waste from blood?
mcq.explain: Kidneys filter blood and make urine.
Carbs, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, water — all six.
NCDs like diabetes are not passed person-to-person.
Regular activity strengthens heart, muscles, and mind.
Clean hands and body prevent disease spread.

flashcards.back: Right amounts from all food groups
Vaccines train body to resist disease

mcq.explain: Light travels in straight lines until something changes its path.

choices: Light bounces off them into our eyes
mcq.explain: Reflected light enters our eyes and forms the image.
Sound is a vibration that needs a medium (air, water, solids).
Smooth shiny surfaces reflect most of the light.
Sound bounces off a distant surface back to you.
A prism splits white light into red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet.

mcq.q: A pure substance is made of…
mcq.explain: Pure substances (like pure water) contain only one kind of particle.

mcq.explain: Filtering, sieving, evaporation, magnetism can separate mixtures.
Salt dissolves fully in water → a solution.

mcq.q: Which method separates iron from sand?
mcq.explain: Magnetism attracts iron but not sand.
Heating makes liquid turn to vapour, leaving the solid behind.
Sugar is soluble — it dissolves in water.

mcq.explain: A hypothesis is an educated guess that can be tested.
Change one variable, keep others the same.

mcq.q: The variable you change is the…
mcq.explain: The independent variable is the cause you change.

mcq.q: What is the variable you measure?
mcq.explain: The dependent variable is the effect you measure.

mcq.q: Data recorded during an experiment should be…
mcq.explain: Real scientists record exactly what they observe.

mcq.q: The last step of the scientific method is…

mcq.explain: Speed = distance travelled ÷ time taken.

mcq.q: A runner covers 100 m in 20 s. Speed =

mcq.explain: If forces balance, the object stays still or moves at constant speed.
Earth's gravity pulls each kg of mass with about 10 N (9.8 N precisely).
Rougher = more friction.

mcq.q: A parachute slows a fall using…
mcq.explain: Air resistance pushes up against the falling parachute.

mcq.q: The three main rock types are…
mcq.explain: Rocks form in three ways, creating three main types.
Sand, shells, and silt settle and compress over long periods.
'Igneous' comes from Latin for fire — molten rock cooled.
Bauxite (used to make aluminium) is mined in many parishes.

choices: Rock bits, organic matter, water, air
mcq.explain: Soil has weathered rocks, humus, water, and air.
Humus makes soil fertile — great for plants.

Green plants capture sunlight via photosynthesis.

mcq.q: Grass → Rabbit → Fox. The fox is the…
mcq.explain: Rabbit eats grass (primary); fox eats rabbit (secondary).

mcq.q: Removing a top predator can cause…
mcq.explain: Without predators, prey numbers may explode — upsetting balance.

mcq.q: In a Jamaican coral reef, producers are…
mcq.explain: Algae and seagrass are the base of the marine food web.
More biodiversity usually means a healthier ecosystem.

11 has only two factors: 1 and 11.
The set has 4 elements.

Translation slides a shape without rotating.
Reflection = mirror flip across a line.
Rotation keeps shape same but orientation changes.

mcq.explain: One of two equally likely outcomes.

'Out of Many, One People' honours all ethnic contributions.

mcq.explain: Apple isn't a Jamaican symbol. National fruit = ackee.
Gold=sun/wealth; Black=strength/creativity; Green=land.

mcq.explain: Heritage Week / National Heroes Day = October.

mcq.explain: Communities thrive when people work together and respect each other.

Rights belong to every person — food, school, speech, safety.
Talking openly and compromising helps solve conflicts.

mcq.explain: CARICOM aids trade, education, disaster response.

mcq.explain: Speak up, support, and create positive culture.

mcq.explain: Trees anchor soil and absorb water; removing them worsens erosion and floods.

mcq.q: A way to reduce plastic pollution is…
mcq.explain: The 3 Rs cut waste at the source.

mcq.q: Mining bauxite helps Jamaica economically but can…
mcq.explain: Mining without rehabilitation harms soils and biodiversity.
Warming and pollution bleach corals.
Use resources so future people can too.

We depend on each other for trade, ideas, support.
These are Jamaica's major export earners.
Jamaica imports what it doesn't produce locally.
Culture sharing enriches societies.

mcq.explain: Reggae, jerk, Patois, and sprinters are world-famous.

Shared beliefs, customs, art of a people

Growing connection of world cultures and economies

mcq.q: The main aim of listening is…
mcq.explain: Listening seeks to understand — not just hear noise.
A strong opener grabs attention.

mcq.q: Formal speech tone is used when…
mcq.explain: Formal = respectful, structured language.
Phones distract — put them away.
Paraphrasing proves understanding.
The same words can mean different things with different tones.

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Listening & Speaking

choices: Slowly with pauses at every word
mcq.explain: Fluency = smooth, accurate, expressive reading.
Common words recognised instantly (the, and, was).
Surrounding words give clues to meaning.
Re-, un-, dis- are added to the front.
Prefix 'un-' = not.
Expression conveys meaning to the audience.

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Fluency & Recognition

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Fluency & Recognition

choices: The key message of the text
mcq.explain: Main idea = the core point, supported by details.
You read between the lines using evidence and experience.
Traits are qualities (kind, brave, shy) shown through actions.
PIE — Persuade, Inform, Entertain.
Fiction comes from imagination.
Non-fiction is based on real facts and events.

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Reading Comprehension

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Reading Comprehension

mcq.explain: The subject is who or what performs the action.

choices: Two complete thoughts with a conjunction
mcq.explain: E.g., 'I went home, and I slept.' Two complete ideas joined.
Questions end with a question mark.
Sound alike, different meanings and spellings.
Can't = cannot; apostrophe shows missing letters.
Proper nouns name specific places, people — capitalised.

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Advanced Grammar

choices: An original document or firsthand account
mcq.explain: Primary = original (diaries, letters, interviews).
Textbooks and articles that discuss primary sources.

mcq.q: To verify information online, you should…
mcq.explain: Cross-check facts on reliable sites.
Always cite your sources.
Biased news favours one side.

mcq.explain: All channels that deliver information.

mcq.q: When nervous before a speech, you can…
mcq.explain: Deep breathing and rehearsal calm nerves.
Sleep and review boost performance.

mcq.q: On a multiple-choice test, you should…
mcq.explain: The best answer may be C or D — read them all.
Structured essays are clear and persuasive.

mcq.q: Key words in a question are…
choices: Words that tell you what to do
mcq.explain: 'Compare', 'describe', 'list' tell you the task.

mcq.q: If you finish a test early, you should…
mcq.explain: Review to catch errors.

mcq.explain: Brave and courageous have the same meaning.
Opposite of generous is selfish.
From Latin 'bene' = good, 'volens' = willing.

mcq.q: Which best fits: 'The jerk chicken had a ___ aroma.'
mcq.explain: Aromas are captivating, tantalising — teasing the senses.

Idiom for saying something ironically or humorously.

mcq.q: If all Jamaicans love ackee and Kemar is Jamaican, Kemar probably…
mcq.explain: Applying a general rule to a specific case = deduction.
Good arguments use evidence.
Cause → effect. E.g., rain → wet roads.

choices: Jamaica is an island in the Caribbean
mcq.explain: Facts can be verified; the rest are opinions.
Critical readers question and evaluate.

mcq.q: Which is NOT a logical fallacy example?
mcq.explain: Evidence is the opposite of a fallacy.
